meaning: n.鎶芥悙,鍝勫爞澶х瑧,绀句細鍔ㄤ贡
examples: Thirteen per cent said they became unconscious at night and 5 per cent suffered convulsions.
This unexpected result threw the company into convulsions of merriment.
Venkateshwarlu ticks off the effects of overexposure: diarrhea, nausea, difficulty in breathing, convulsions, headaches and depression.
An overdose may produce slow and shallow breathing, clammy skin, convulsions, coma and death.
